relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jethro hears what YHWH has done in liberation.
Zipporah returns into the story.
Gershom remembers sojourning.
Eliezer remembers Divine help and rescue.
Family comes to Moses at the mountain of Divine.
Jethro announces wife and sons coming.
Moses meets him with honor, kiss, peace, and tent hospitality.
Moses tells the whole rescue story.
Jethro rejoices in the good YHWH did for Israel.
Jethro blesses YHWH, rescuer from Pharaoh's hand.
He recognizes YHWH's greatness over arrogant powers.
Jethro offers, and Israel's elders eat bread before Divine.
Next day Moses sits alone under the weight of every case.
Jethro sees the bottleneck and asks why Moses sits alone.
Moses says the people come to inquire of Divine.
He judges matters and teaches statutes and instruction.
Jethro says plainly: this is not good.
You and the people will wear out; the matter is too heavy alone.
Counsel comes with blessing: may Divine be with you.
Teach the way to walk and work to do.
Choose capable, reverent, truthful people who hate unjust gain.
Let them bear with you and make the weight lighter.
Then you can stand, and the people can go in peace.
Moses listens to Jethro's voice.
Leadership is distributed among capable people.
Hard matters come to Moses; smaller matters are handled nearby.
Jethro returns to his land after giving wisdom.

contemplative_note: Exodus 18 is sacred delegation: even the liberator cannot carry everything alone. Wisdom from outside the group protects leader and people from collapse.
